key_insight: Across 15 foundation-model backbones, mammography-specific models performed best on out-of-distribution mammography tasks, but robustness depended on the dataset rather than mammography pretraining alone.
clinical_relevance: This suggests that choosing imaging features for breast cancer support tools should prioritize external validation across multiple sites and datasets, not just in-domain accuracy.
method: Benchmarked 15 frozen foundation-model backbones with a linear probe.
Trained on 3 source datasets and tested on 12 label-harmonized OOD datasets.
Compared performance across breast density, BI-RADS severity, and cancer status.
evidence_signal: The study used a unified frozen-backbone, linear-probe protocol with multi-dataset OOD testing, revealing heterogeneous dataset-level performance even for top models.
watch_item: Results may not transfer reliably without dataset-level external validation, since strong average OOD performance did not guarantee consistent performance across individual datasets.
paper_title: Benchmarking the Robustness of Foundation Models for Mammography under Domain Shift
